name: Chrishan Fernando
title: Senior Systems Engineer
tagline: Regulated MedTech · Safety-critical systems · Requirements & V&V
summary: Twelve years engineering safety-critical systems — from radiology AI platforms in regulated MedTech to embedded defence systems. Most recently at Harrison.ai, leading systems engineering for the Harrison Open Platform, a multi-tenant platform delivering radiology AI results into hospitals worldwide. My work is the engineering around the system — requirements, interfaces, integration and safety — not building the models themselves. I care about building technology that makes a real difference to patients and the clinicians who care for them.
about: I'm a systems engineer: my work sits around the AI, not inside it. I treat the models as components — managing the interfaces, requirements, integration and clinical safety of the system that wraps them. At Harrison.ai I owned system-wide requirements, led patient and security risk assessments for SaMD/AI components, and served as audit SME for MDSAP — defending design and development practices to keep the platform compliant with ISO 13485, ISO 14971 and IEC 62304.

Before MedTech I spent six years in defence at Thales and Boeing: flight simulators, air traffic management, command-and-control systems, and tactical data links. I've written C for collision-avoidance systems, Python for navigational data analysis, and lately a lot of specs for AI to write the code for me.
Outside of work I tinker — mostly with AI-assisted development (Claude Code, OpenSpec), healthcare interoperability (SMART on FHIR), and self-hosted tools that scratch my own itches.

education.degree: B.E. Mechatronics / B.Com (Finance), First Class Honours
education.notes: Merit Scholarship · Major Industrial Project Placement Scholarship
